title: Cherleria eglandulosa

Cherleria eglandulosa is native to the western United States and is often found in dry, sandy areas. The plant's classification is further supported by its characteristics, such as its small, white flowers and narrow, pointed leaves. Cherleria eglandulosa is also known to be closely related to other species within the Brassicaceae family.
